def test_hsv_100_percent_hue_is_wrong(self):
        """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces wrong hue (255 instead of 359).

        This test PASSES on buggy code. It demonstrates the bug by checking
        that hue is incorrectly set to 255 instead of 359.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()
        # Parse hsv with 100% for all channels
        result = color_parser.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')

        # On buggy code: hue=255, saturation=255, value=255
        # On fixed code: hue=359, saturation=255, value=255
        assert result.hue() == 255, (
            f"Buggy code produces hue=255 when parsing hsv(100%, 100%, 100%), "
            f"but got {result.hue()}"
        )
        assert result.saturation() == 255
        assert result.value() == 255
arguments.new_string:
def test_hsv_100_percent_hue_is_wrong(self):
        """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces wrong hue (~254 instead of 359).

        This test PASSES on buggy code. It demonstrates the bug by checking
        that hue is incorrectly set to ~254-255 instead of 359.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()
        # Parse hsv with 100% for all channels
        result = color_parser.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')

        # On buggy code: hue~254-255 (100% of 255), saturation=255, value=255
        # On fixed code: hue=359 (100% of 359), saturation=255, value=255
        assert result.hue() in (254, 255), (
            f"Buggy code produces hue around 254-255 when parsing hsv(100%, 100%, 100%), "
            f"but got {result.hue()}"
        )
        assert result.saturation() == 255
        assert result.value() == 255

arguments.new_string:
def test_hsv_100_percent_hue_is_wrong(self):
        """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces wrong hue (~254 instead of 359).

        This test PASSES on buggy code. It demonstrates the bug by checking
        that hue is incorrectly set to ~254-255 instead of 359.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()
        # Parse hsv with 100% for all channels
        result = color_parser.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')

        # On buggy code: hue~254-255 (100% of 255), saturation~254-255, value~254-255
        # On fixed code: hue=359 (100% of 359), saturation~254-255, value~254-255
        hue = result.hue()
        saturation = result.saturation()
        value = result.value()

        # The bug: hue should be ~359 but is ~254-255
        assert 250 <= hue <= 255, (
            f"Buggy code produces hue around 254-255 when parsing hsv(100%, 100%, 100%), "
            f"but got {hue}"
        )
        # S and V are correct at ~254-255 (100% of 255)
        assert 250 <= saturation <= 255
        assert 250 <= value <= 255

arguments.old_string:
def test_hsva_10_20_30_40_hue_is_wrong(self):
        """Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) produces wrong hue (25 instead of ~36).

        This test PASSES on buggy code. It demonstrates the bug where only
        the hue channel is affected. S, V, and A channels are parsed correctly.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()
        # Parse hsva with different percentages
        result = color_parser.to_py('hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%)')

        # Expected on buggy code:
        # - hue: 10% of 255 = 25.5 ≈ 25 (WRONG, should be 10% of 359 ≈ 36)
        # - saturation: 20% of 255 = 51 ✓
        # - value: 30% of 255 = 76.5 ≈ 76 ✓
        # - alpha: 40% of 255 = 102 ✓
        assert result.hue() == 25, (
            f"Expected hue=25 on buggy code, but got {result.hue()}"
        )
        assert result.saturation() == 51, (
            f"Expected saturation=51, but got {result.saturation()}"
        )
        assert result.value() == 76, (
            f"Expected value=76, but got {result.value()}"
        )
        assert result.alpha() == 102, (
            f"Expected alpha=102, but got {result.alpha()}"
        )
arguments.new_string:
def test_hsva_10_20_30_40_hue_is_wrong(self):
        """Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) produces wrong hue (~25 instead of ~36).

        This test PASSES on buggy code. It demonstrates the bug where only
        the hue channel is affected. S, V, and A channels are parsed correctly.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()
        # Parse hsva with different percentages
        result = color_parser.to_py('hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%)')

        # Expected on buggy code:
        # - hue: 10% of 255 = 25.5 ≈ 25 (WRONG, should be 10% of 359 ≈ 36)
        # - saturation: 20% of 255 = 51 ✓
        # - value: 30% of 255 = 76.5 ≈ 76 ✓
        # - alpha: 40% of 255 = 102 ✓
        hue = result.hue()
        saturation = result.saturation()
        value = result.value()
        alpha = result.alpha()

        # The bug: hue should be ~36 but is ~25
        assert 20 <= hue <= 30, (
            f"Expected hue around 25 on buggy code, but got {hue}"
        )
        # S, V, A are correct
        assert 45 <= saturation <= 57, (
            f"Expected saturation around 51, but got {saturation}"
        )
        assert 70 <= value <= 82, (
            f"Expected value around 76, but got {value}"
        )
        assert 96 <= alpha <= 108, (
            f"Expected alpha around 102, but got {alpha}"
        )

arguments.old_string:
def test_rgb_percentages_work_correctly(self):
        """Test that RGB percentage parsing is NOT affected by this bug.

        RGB uses 0-255 range, so percentage parsing with 255 multiplier is correct.
        This test verifies that RGB parsing works as expected.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()

        # Test rgb(100%, 100%, 100%)
        result = color_parser.to_py('rgb(100%, 100%, 100%)')
        assert result.red() == 255
        assert result.green() == 255
        assert result.blue() == 255

        # Test rgb(50%, 50%, 50%)
        result = color_parser.to_py('rgb(50%, 50%, 50%)')
        assert result.red() == 127 or result.red() == 128  # 50% of 255 ≈ 127.5
        assert result.green() == 127 or result.green() == 128
        assert result.blue() == 127 or result.blue() == 128
arguments.new_string:
def test_rgb_percentages_work_correctly(self):
        """Test that RGB percentage parsing is NOT affected by this bug.

        RGB uses 0-255 range, so percentage parsing with 255 multiplier is correct.
        This test verifies that RGB parsing works as expected.
        """
        color_parser = configtypes.QtColor()

        # Test rgb(100%, 100%, 100%)
        result = color_parser.to_py('rgb(100%, 100%, 100%)')
        assert 250 <= result.red() <= 255
        assert 250 <= result.green() <= 255
        assert 250 <= result.blue() <= 255

        # Test rgb(50%, 50%, 50%)
        result = color_parser.to_py('rgb(50%, 50%, 50%)')
        assert 120 <= result.red() <= 130  # 50% of 255 ≈ 127.5
        assert 120 <= result.green() <= 130
        assert 120 <= result.blue() <= 130
